title: padus avium subsp. pubescens
intro: Padus avium subsp. pubescens, also known as the downy dogwood or American dogwood, is a plant species in the family Rosaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Padus avium subsp. pubescens include its ability to reduce fever, relieve digestive issues, and soothe skin irritations. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
